title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: vignette provides end--end workflow LakeEnsemblR.WQ: Configure coupled model inputs. Validate model setups. Run model ensembles. Compute harmonized metrics. Prepare run calibration. Run sensitivity analysis.

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: physical LakeEnsemblR setup already exported. water-quality config file: LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml. metrics config file: Output.yaml. GLM-AED2 GOTM-WET GOTM-Selmaprotbas SIMSTRAT-AED2
code: library(LakeEnsemblR) library(LakeEnsemblR.WQ)

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
code: export_config_wq(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder = ".",   verbose = TRUE )  export_inputs(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder = "." )

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
code: validate_glm_aed(config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml", folder = ".") validate_gotm_wet(config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml", folder = ".") validate_simstrat(config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml", folder = ".")

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
code: run_res <- run_ensemble_wq(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   models = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "SIMSTRAT-AED2"),   folder = ".",   validate = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
code: metric_out <- cal_metrics(   metric_yaml_file = "Output.yaml",   model_filter = "all",   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" )  stat_out <- cal_stats(metric_out)

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: create_calibration_tables() creates: - one master file module/model parameters - one editable file per active module Edit module files calibration folder: - set include = TRUE selected parameters - adjust lower, upper, initial needed
code: create_calibration_tables(   folder = ".",   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder_out = "calibration",   models_coupled = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "SIMSTRAT-AED2"),   bounds_factor = 0.2 )

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
code: cs_all <- calib_setup_from_tables(   folder_in = "calibration",   model_coupled = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "SIMSTRAT-AED2") )

what: 5.3 Run small LHC test per model
title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: Run one model time.
code: cs_glm <- subset(cs_all, model_coupled == "GLM-AED2")  res_glm <- run_lhc_wq(   model = "GLM-AED2",   param_names = unique(cs_glm$pars),   calib_setup = cs_glm,   yaml_file = "Output.yaml",   model_dir = "GLM-AED2",   n_samples = 3,   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   verbose = TRUE )

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
code: res_sens <- run_sensitivity(   param_name = "theta_sed_oxy",   calib_setup = cs_glm,   yaml_file = "Output.yaml",   model_dir = "GLM-AED2",   n_steps = 10,   model_filter = "GLM" )

what: No metrics found for selected model
title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: Use coupling-specific model_filter values metrics extraction: - GLM-AED2 -> GLM - GOTM-WET -> WET - GOTM-Selmaprotbas -> SELMAPROTBAS - SIMSTRAT-AED2 -> SIMSTRAT

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ Full Workflow
text: Calibration tables may store dictionary-style paths (example, section/parameter) instead physical filenames. Current run_lhc_wq updates dictionary paths GLM-AED2, GOTM-WET, GOTM-Selmaprotbas, SIMSTRAT-AED2.

code: @Manual{,   title = {LakeEnsemblR.WQ: Run Ensembles of Water Quality 1D Lake Models},   author = {Authors@R: c and email = "tbo@ecos.au.dk") and {person} and {person} and {person)}},   year = {2026},   note = {https://github.com/aemon-j/LakeEnsemblR.WQ,     https://aemon-j.github.io/LakeEnsemblR.WQ}, }

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: Run, harmonize, compare water quality simulations across multiple 1D lake model frameworks. LakeEnsemblR.WQ extends LakeEnsemblR workflows tools : configure model-specific WQ settings, validate model setups run, run model ensembles, calibrate model ensembles, extract harmonize output variables, calculate standardized ecosystem metrics summary statistics. Supported model frameworks: GLM-AED2 GOTM-WET GOTM-Selmaprotbas Simstrat-AED2

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
code: # install.packages("remotes") # After release it will be pushed to aemon-j github account. remotes::install_github("tubabucak/LakeEnsemblR.WQ")

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
code: library(LakeEnsemblR)  # 0) Export model-specific configuration and inputs for physical setup  export_config(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR.yaml",   folder = ".",   verbose = TRUE )  library(LakeEnsemblR.WQ)   # 1) Export model-specific configuration and inputs for WQ setup export_config_wq(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder = ".",   verbose = TRUE )  # 2) Run selected models run_res <- run_ensemble_wq(   config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   models = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "SIMSTRAT-AED2"),   folder = ".",   validate = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )  # 3) Compute metrics listed in Output.yaml metric_out <- cal_metrics(   metric_yaml_file = "Output.yaml",   model_filter = "all",   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" )  # 4) Optional: compute summary statistics stat_out <- cal_stats(metric_out)

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: export_config_wq() export_inputs() set_up_configs() set_value_config() validate_glm_aed() validate_gotm_wet() validate_simstrat() run_ensemble_wq() get_output_wq() extract_variable_list() cal_metrics() cal_stats() export_all_stats() run_lhc_wq()

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: Common project files include: LakeEnsemblR_WQ master config (model/module settings) LakeEnsemblR physical config Output metric config (selected metric families) Example typical setup: LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml LakeEnsemblR.yaml Output.yaml LakeEnsemblR_bathymetry_standard LakeEnsemblR_ice-height_standard LakeEnsemblR_inflow_standard LakeEnsemblR_meteo_standard LakeEnsemblR_outflow_standard

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: cal_metrics() returns nested list metric model. metric/model combination, values stored data frames datetime depth/value columns. Example access:
code: metric_out$GLM$TP_gramsPerCubicMeter

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: model run succeeds metrics missing: Confirm metric enabled Output.yaml. Confirm metric-variable mapping exists metrics dictionary. Confirm model actually wrote variable output. Simstrat-AED2 specifically, missing derived metrics (example total chlorophyll-total phosphorus) often caused missing output variables Simstrat/AED2 output configuration (OutputDiagnosticVars ‘true’ ), run failure.

title: LakeEnsemblR.WQ
text: Fork repository. Clone fork. Add original repository upstream. Pull latest changes upstream/main. Create feature branch, commit changes, push. Open pull request. Example git commands:
code: git clone git@github.com:<username>/LakeEnsemblR.WQ.git cd LakeEnsemblR.WQ git remote add upstream git@github.com:aemon-j/LakeEnsemblR.WQ.git git fetch upstream git checkout -b my-feature git merge upstream/main git push -u origin my-feature

what: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
title: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
text: Reads per-module calibration CSV files produced create_calibration_tables, filters rows include == TRUE, returns calib_setup data frame format expected run_lhc_wq run_sensitivity.

title: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
code: calib_setup_from_tables(folder_in, model_coupled, group_name = NULL)

title: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
text: folder_in character; path folder containing calibration_<module>.csv files (folder_out used create_calibration_tables). model_coupled character; one coupled model name (e.g. "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "GLM-AED2"), character vector coupled model names. parameters models included output. group_name character NULL; biological modules multiple groups (phytoplankton, zooplankton, etc.) maps group_name column expected run_lhc_wq. NULL (default) column set NA.

title: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
text: data frame columns model_coupled, module,   pars, lb, ub, x0, log, file,   group_name, unit, note. single model   supplied, result can passed directly run_lhc_wq   run_sensitivity. multiple models supplied, output   combined reference table filtered per model running   calibration.

title: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
text: function looks files matching calibration_*.csv inside folder_in, stacks , filters include == TRUE requested model_coupled, renames columns match calib_setup contract used calibration runners. multiple models supplied, returned data frame includes model_coupled rows different model couplings remain distinguishable.

title: Build calib_setup from edited calibration CSVs — calib_setup_from_tables
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # After editing calibration CSVs to set include = TRUE: calib_setup <- calib_setup_from_tables(   folder_in     = "calibration",   model_coupled = "GOTM-Selmaprotbas" )  results <- run_lhc_wq(   model          = "GOTM-Selmaprotbas",   param_names    = calib_setup$pars,   calib_setup    = calib_setup,   yaml_file      = "metrics.yaml",   model_dir      = "GOTM-Selmaprotbas",   n_samples      = 50,   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" ) } # }

title: Calculate Model Performance Statistics — cal_stats
text: Computes statistical performance metrics evaluate model predictions observations.

title: Calculate Model Performance Statistics — cal_stats
code: cal_stats(observed, predicted)

title: Calculate Model Performance Statistics — cal_stats
text: observed Numeric vector observed values (e.g., observed concentrations mg/L). predicted Numeric vector predicted values (e.g., modeled concentrations mg/L).

title: Calculate Model Performance Statistics — cal_stats
text: list containing: residual Residuals (observed - predicted) NSE Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency RMSE Root Mean Squared Error NRMSE Normalized RMSE PBIAS Percent Bias lnlikelihood Log-likelihood KGE Kling-Gupta Efficiency

title: Calculate Model Performance Statistics — cal_stats
text: function first removes NA values calculates following metrics: NSE: Nash-Sutcliffe Efficiency RMSE: Root Mean Squared Error NRMSE: Normalized Root Mean Squared Error (normalized range observed values) PBIAS: Percent Bias lnlikelihood: Log-likelihood assuming normal distribution KGE: Kling-Gupta Efficiency (hydroGOF::KGE) residual: Vector observed - predicted residuals small observed predicted values (< 1e-3), minimum threshold applied avoid division near-zero values PBIAS calculation.

text: temp_data dataframe; output model/observed output. include Datetime depth specific values per column. hemisphere character; N Northern Hemisphere, S Southern hemisphere

title: Calculate Stratified Days — cal_strat_date
text: dataframe starting date stratification, start date mixing duration stratification

title: Convert LakeEnsemblR configuration to LakeEnsemblR.WQ — convert_ler_to_lerwq
code: convert_ler_to_lerwq(   ler_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR.yaml",   lerwq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder = ".",   copy_folder = TRUE,   rename_folders = FALSE,   activate_wq = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Convert LakeEnsemblR configuration to LakeEnsemblR.WQ — convert_ler_to_lerwq
text: ler_config_file character; name LakeEnsemblR config file lerwq_config_file character; name LakeEnsemblR_WQ config file folder path; location configuration files copy_folder logical; true, copies folders LakeEnsemblR run rename_folders logical; true, rename LakeEnsemblR folders able activate_wq logical; true, changes parameters run WQ verbose logical; true, prints messages

title: Create calibration tables — create_calibration_tables
text: Generates master calibration CSV per-module CSVs LakeEnsemblR.WQ dictionary. Every parameter gets include = FALSE default users can review selectively opt-. Lower upper bounds set default * (1 - bounds_factor) default * (1 + bounds_factor).

title: Create calibration tables — create_calibration_tables
code: create_calibration_tables(   folder = ".",   config_file,   folder_out = folder,   models_coupled = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "GOTM-WET", "Simstrat-AED2"),   bounds_factor = 0.2 )

title: Create calibration tables — create_calibration_tables
text: folder path; directory containing config file. config_file character; name LakeEnsemblR_WQ YAML config file. folder_out path; output directory CSV files (created needed). models_coupled character vector; model couplings include. bounds_factor numeric; fractional deviation default bounds (default = 0.2, .e. ±20%).

title: Create calibration tables — create_calibration_tables
text: Invisibly returns master calibration table data frame.

title: Create calibration tables — create_calibration_tables
text: Workflow: Run create_calibration_tables() — generates     calibration_master.csv (read-reference) one     calibration_<module>.csv per active module. Open per-module CSVs, set include = TRUE parameters     want calibrate, adjust lower / upper /     initial needed. Call calib_setup_from_tables read edited CSVs     build calib_setup data frame expected     run_lhc_wq run_sensitivity.

title: Create calibration tables — create_calibration_tables
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ create_calibration_tables(   folder         = ".",   config_file    = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml",   folder_out     = "calibration",   models_coupled = c("GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "GLM-AED2"),   bounds_factor  = 0.2 ) # Then edit calibration/calibration_<module>.csv files, # set include = TRUE for chosen parameters, and run: calib_setup <- calib_setup_from_tables(folder_in = "calibration",                                        model_coupled = "GOTM-Selmaprotbas") } # }

what: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
text: Performs Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) based parameter space exploration water quality models coupled lake hydrodynamic models. Supports GLM-AED2, GOTM-WET, GOTM-Selmaprotbas, Simstrat-AED2.

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
code: .cal_lhc_obs_stats(   obs_data,   dict,   model_short,   yaml_file,   wq_config_file = NULL )

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
text: yaml_file Character. Path YAML metrics file passed cal_metrics. wq_config_file Character. Path WQ config file passed cal_metrics. model Character. One "GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "Simstrat-AED2" (case-insensitive). param_names Character vector. Names parameters vary (must match pars column calib_setup). calib_setup Data frame. Calibration setup table minimum columns: pars (parameter names), lb (lower bound), ub (upper bound), file (path relative model_dir), optionally group_name (e.g. phytoplankton groups CSV parameter files). model_dir Character. Path model simulation directory. n_samples Integer. Number LHS samples (model runs) perform (default = 50). model_filter Character. Optional model filter string passed cal_metrics. NULL (default), auto-derived model (e.g., "GLM-AED2" → "GLM", "GOTM-WET" → "WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas" → "SELMAPROTBAS"). yaml_file_model Character. Name GOTM yaml file used running GOTM-based models (default = "gotm.yaml"). Ignored GLM-AED2 Simstrat-AED2. par_file Character. Name Simstrat .par file (default = "simstrat.par"). Ignored models. verbose Logical. Print progress messages (default = TRUE). save_results Logical. TRUE, save results .rds file inside model_dir iterations (default = FALSE). output_file Character. File name saved results save_results = TRUE (default = "lhc_results.rds"). obs_file Character NULL. Path standard observed data CSV columns datetime, depth, variable_global_name, value (values global units defined metrics dictionary). provided, model run evaluated observations flat data.frame parameter values plus performance statistics returned (default = NULL).

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
text: obs_file = NULL: list length n_samples.   element list : params Named list sampled parameter values iteration. metrics Output cal_metrics() iteration. model_ok Logical; TRUE model run produced output. obs_file provided: data.frame row one successful LHC iteration. Columns sampled parameter values followed performance statistics (NSE, RMSE, NRMSE, PBIAS, KGE) every variable–depth combination present observed data.

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
text: LHS iteration function: Samples parameter values bounds defined calib_setup. Writes sampled values relevant .nml .csv files. Runs selected model. Extracts metrics via cal_metrics.

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — .cal_lhc_obs_stats
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_lhc_wq(   model       = "GLM-AED2",   param_names = c("Kw", "Knitrif"),   calib_setup = calib_setup,   yaml_file   = "metrics.yaml",   model_dir   = "GLM-AED2",   n_samples   = 30,   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" )  results <- run_lhc_wq(   model       = "GOTM-Selmaprotbas",   param_names = c("spm_initial", "p_vel"),   calib_setup = calib_setup,   yaml_file   = "metrics.yaml",   model_dir   = "GOTM-Selmaprotbas",   n_samples   = 50,   wq_config_file = "LakeEnsemblR_WQ.yaml" ) } # }

what: Check and Extract Selected Metrics from output.YAML — .load_metrics_dictionary_wq
title: Check and Extract Selected Metrics from output.YAML — .load_metrics_dictionary_wq
text: function reads YAML file containing metrics extracts relevant metric details based provided dictionary. Firstly, checks whether metric YAML file exists dictionary , extracts necessary information needed calculating metric.

title: Check and Extract Selected Metrics from output.YAML — .load_metrics_dictionary_wq
code: .load_metrics_dictionary_wq(dict_file = NULL, metric_yaml_file = NULL)

title: Check and Extract Selected Metrics from output.YAML — .load_metrics_dictionary_wq
text: dict_file Character data.frame: Metric dictionary source. Can full file path (.rda .csv), data.frame, NULL. NULL/empty, bundled default dictionary used. metric_yaml_file Character: Name YAML file containing list metrics.

what: Format a Simstrat-style AED2 inflow file (character vector) — format_aed_inflow_simstrat
title: Format a Simstrat-style AED2 inflow file (character vector) — format_aed_inflow_simstrat
text: helper creates contents Simstrat inflow file single AED2 variable, given time axis simple inflow geometry (deep vs surface inflows). mirrors structure format_flow_simstrat() used Q/T/S inflows, applied AED2 tracers.

title: Format a Simstrat-style AED2 inflow file (character vector) — format_aed_inflow_simstrat
code: format_aed_inflow_simstrat(   varname,   levels,   surf_flow,   sim_par = "simstrat.par",   times = NULL,   default_value = 0,   unit = "millimolesPerMeterCubed" )

title: Format a Simstrat-style AED2 inflow file (character vector) — format_aed_inflow_simstrat
text: varname Character; AED2 inflow variable name, e.g. "PHS_frp_inflow". levels Numeric vector; inflow depths (m), one per branch. surf_flow Logical vector; length levels, TRUE surface inflows, FALSE deep inflows. sim_par Character; path simstrat.par-style JSON config. times Numeric vector times [days] template time series. NULL, Simulation$"Start d" Simulation$"End d" used daily step. default_value Numeric; default value assign depths times (e.g. 0 tracers, 8 CAR_pH_inflow). unit Character; unit string include header, e.g. "millimolesPerMeterCubed" "pH".

title: Format a Simstrat-style AED2 inflow file (character vector) — format_aed_inflow_simstrat
text: character vector lines written .dat file.

what: Generate Simstrat–AED2 inflow files for active modules — generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows
title: Generate Simstrat–AED2 inflow files for active modules — generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows
text: Creates template Simstrat inflow .dat files AED2 variables associated active modules aed2_file. Phytoplankton variables expanded based group names aed2_phyto_pars.nml.

title: Generate Simstrat–AED2 inflow files for active modules — generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows
code: generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows(   aed2_file,   phyto_pars_file = NULL,   sim_par,   out_dir,   levels = NULL,   surf_flow = NULL,   default_value = 0,   unit = "millimolesPerMeterCubed",   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Generate Simstrat–AED2 inflow files for active modules — generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows
text: aed2_file Character; path aed2.nml. phyto_pars_file Character; path aed2_phyto_pars.nml. Required aed2_phytoplankton active. sim_par Character; path simstrat.par-style JSON config. out_dir Character; directory inflow .dat files written. levels Numeric vector; inflow depths (m). NULL together surf_flow, single surface inflow 0 m assumed. surf_flow Logical vector; TRUE surface inflows, FALSE deep inflows. Must length levels. default_value Numeric; default initial inflow value (0 tracers). unit Character; default unit string use non-pH variables. inflow_map Data frame mapping AED2 modules inflow variable base names; defaults [aed2_inflow_map].

title: Generate Simstrat–AED2 inflow files for active modules — generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows
text: Invisibly returns character vector inflow variable names   files written (without .dat extension).

title: Generate Simstrat–AED2 inflow files for active modules — generate_simstrat_aed2_inflows
text: default, inflows treated surface inflows 0 m, variables initialised default_value, except CAR_pH_inflow, initialised 8 unit "pH".

what: Calculate & export performance metrics for integrated samples - so far only for specific depths — integrate_depths
title: Calculate & export performance metrics for integrated samples - so far only for specific depths — integrate_depths
text: function integrates selected variable (e.g., , temperature) across specified depths model output, compares observed data, returns summary statistics. aim function integrate model outputs across specified depth intervals since observed water sample data often collected integrated samples multiple depths. two options function: 1)user either specify exact depth 2)user give provides depth range vector (e.g., `c(lower, upper)`) 3)user gives dataframe dynamic lower-upper bounds certain period. dataframe columns datetime, lower, upper function integrate average values within depts.

title: Calculate & export performance metrics for integrated samples - so far only for specific depths — integrate_depths
code: export_all_stats(output, variable, depths, bathy_path, obs_data)  integrate_depths(   output,   depth,   variable,   bathy_path,   obs_data = NULL,   folder = NULL,   hypso_avg = TRUE,   dz = 0.1 )

title: Calculate & export performance metrics for integrated samples - so far only for specific depths — integrate_depths
text: output List: list containing model output data different models different depths. variable character: variable name extracted depths Numeric vector: One depths extract integrate . far just works single vector output bathy_path character: variable name extracted obs_data list: list observed data depth numeric vector dataframe: 1)exact depth extracted, provide simple numeric value, 2)integrating across depths time series, provide vector c(lower, upper) 3) want integrate different depth intervals different dates, dataframe datetime, lower, upper values given folder character: main filepath; output stored hypso_avg logical: TRUE= hypsometric curve (depth-area relationship) used, otherwise average selected columns calculated dz numeric: interpolation depth interval. default 0.1

title: Calculate & export performance metrics for integrated samples - so far only for specific depths — integrate_depths
text: data frame calculated statistics (e.g., RMSE, KGE) model, depth, variable. list integrated variables specified depth depth range.

LER_config_file Full path metric output YAML file. model_folders named list full paths model output folders (e.g., GLM, WET, SELMA).

what: Run an ensemble of LakeEnsemblR.WQ models selected — run_ensemble_wq
title: Run an ensemble of LakeEnsemblR.WQ models selected — run_ensemble_wq
text: Runs selected models via runner packages, optionally skipping invalid models warning, optionally post-processes produced NetCDF outputs.

title: Run an ensemble of LakeEnsemblR.WQ models selected — run_ensemble_wq
code: run_ensemble_wq(   config_file,   models = c("GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "SIMSTRAT-AED2"),   folder = ".",   parallel = FALSE,   ncores = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   on_error = c("skip", "stop"),   validate = TRUE,   post_process = FALSE )

title: Run an ensemble of LakeEnsemblR.WQ models selected — run_ensemble_wq
text: config_file Character. Path master LakeEnsebmlerWQ.yaml configuration file models Character vector. Models run (e.g. c("GLM-AED","GOTM-WET","GOTM_SELMA","SIMSTRAT-AED2")). folder Character. Base folder containing model run directories parallel Logical. Run parallel (implemented yet). ncores Integer. Number cores (used yet). verbose Logical. Print progress messages. on_error Character. "skip" (default) skips invalid/failed models warnings, "stop" aborts first invalid/failed model. validate Logical. TRUE, run model-specific validation first. may important prevent getting error later stage post_process Logical. TRUE, call harmonization/post-processing successful runs. (integrated yet)

title: Run an ensemble of LakeEnsemblR.WQ models selected — run_ensemble_wq
text: list containing validation results, run results, NetCDF paths successful runs.

what: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — run_lhc_wq
title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — run_lhc_wq
text: Performs Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) based parameter-space exploration coupled water-quality model setups evaluates sampled run using either cal_metrics() outputs observed-data statistics.

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — run_lhc_wq
code: run_lhc_wq(   model,   param_names,   calib_setup,   yaml_file,   model_dir,   n_samples = 50,   model_filter = NULL,   wq_config_file = NULL,   yaml_file_model = "gotm.yaml",   par_file = "simstrat.par",   verbose = TRUE,   save_results = FALSE,   output_file = "lhc_results.rds",   obs_file = NULL )

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — run_lhc_wq
text: model Character. One "GLM-AED2", "GOTM-WET", "GOTM-Selmaprotbas", "Simstrat-AED2". param_names Character vector. Parameter names vary. calib_setup Data frame least columns pars, lb, ub, file; optional group_name group-specific CSV updates. yaml_file Character. Path output metrics YAML file. model_dir Character. Path model simulation directory. n_samples Integer. Number LHS samples (default = 50). model_filter Character NULL. Optional model key cal_metrics(). NULL, auto-derived model. wq_config_file Character NULL. Path WQ config file. yaml_file_model Character. GOTM yaml filename (default "gotm.yaml"). par_file Character. Simstrat par filename (default "simstrat.par"). verbose Logical. Print progress messages. save_results Logical. TRUE, save results output_file model_dir. output_file Character. RDS output filename save_results = TRUE. obs_file Character NULL. Optional observed-data CSV per-run statistics.

title: Run Latin Hypercube Calibration for Water Quality Models — run_lhc_wq
text: obs_file = NULL, list length n_samples   sampled parameters metrics per run. obs_file supplied,   returns flattened data frame sampled parameters summary   statistics.

what: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
title: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
text: function performs multi-parameter sensitivity analysis using Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) within specified relative range parameter. modifies input files (either `.nml` `.csv`) lake model, runs model, extracts defined metrics iteration. function reads model configuration files (model directory), applies sampled parameter values, runs lake model using (`run_glm()`), collects metrics using (`cal_metrics()`). supports modifying `.nml` `.csv` files. `.csv`, parameter matched using column named (`pars`), optionally updated using (`group_name`) provided.

title: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
code: run_multi_param_sensitivity(   param_names,   calib_setup,   rel_change,   yaml_file,   model_dir,   n_steps = 10,   model_filter = "GLM" )

title: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
text: param_names character vector parameter names vary sensitivity analysis. calib_setup data frame containing calibration setup information. Must include columns: pars (parameter names), x0 (default values), file (file path relative model_dir), optionally group_name (phytoplankton related parameters). rel_change numeric value specifying relative range (e.g. 0.1 plus minus 10 percent) around default value x0. yaml_file Path YAML file used metric extraction cal_metrics(). model_dir Path directory containing lake model files subdirectories. include parameter files (e.g., phytoplankton). n_steps Number LHS iterations (.e., model realizations). model_filter character string specifying model filter passed cal_metrics() (default "GLM").

title: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
text: list length n_steps, element contains   sampled parameter values extracted metrics.

title: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
text: Parameter sampling based Latin Hypercube Sampling (via (`lhs::randomLHS`)), sampled values scaled symmetric range around (`x0`).

title: Run Multi-Parameter Sensitivity Analysis Using Latin Hypercube Sampling — run_multi_param_sensitivity
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_multi_param_sensitivity(   param_names = c("kw", "sed_temp", "DO_sat"),   calib_setup = calib_setup,   rel_change = 0.1,   yaml_file = "config/metrics.yaml",   model_dir = "GLM",   n_steps = 20 ) } # }

what: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
title: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
text: Performs one---time sensitivity analysis specified model parameter, optionally targeting specific group (e.g., phytoplankton group parameter CSV). parameter value iteratively changed across defined range, model run, selected metrics calculated step.

title: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
code: run_sensitivity(   param_name,   calib_setup,   yaml_file,   model_dir,   n_steps = 10,   model_filter = "GLM",   group_name = NULL )

title: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
text: param_name Character. Name parameter modify (must match `pars` `calib_setup`). calib_setup Data frame. Calibration setup table containing parameter names, bounds (`lb`, `ub`), initial values (`x0`), file names (`file`), optionally `group_name` column group-specific parameters. yaml_file Character. Path YAML file defining metrics extract (output.yaml). model_dir Character. Path model directory input files outputs located. n_steps Integer. Number steps (iterations) parameter value sequence (default = 10). model_filter Character. Model identifier used `cal_metrics()` filter results (default = `"GLM"`). group_name Character `NULL`. Optional certain pyhtoplankton group selected. provided, updates specified group/column parameter CSV (e.g., `"cyano"`, `"green"`, `"diatom"`). `NULL`, relevant rows `calib_setup` `param_name` used.

title: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
text: list one element per parameter value step. element contains: param_value value used iteration. metrics output `cal_metrics()` iteration.

title: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
text: function supports `.nml` `.csv` parameter files. `.csv` files, automatically handles quoted column names parameter names (e.g., `'p_name'`, `'R_growth'`) stripping quotes. `group_name` provided, updates specific group column. Otherwise, columns (column 2 onward) updated matching parameter row.

title: Run Sensitivity Analysis for a Model Parameter — run_sensitivity
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ results <- run_sensitivity("R_growth", calib_setup, yaml_file = "metrics.yaml",                            model_dir = "model/", n_steps = 10, group_name = "cyano") } # }
